step_by_step_thinking:
Cells most commonly affected in glaucomatous optic atrophy?
\boxed{C} Based on the provided evidence, ganglion cells are mentioned frequently as being significantly impacted in various ocular disorders affecting the retina and choroid. Given the focus on glaucomatous optic atrophy, it logically follows that ganglion cells would be one of the primary targets due to their crucial role in transmitting visual information from the retina to the brain.
